checks.reason: The cited plumbing source supports building-level systems, not the narrower classical monumental/civic construction node.
Aqueducts can feed urban plumbing supply but are not required for every building-level plumbing system.
Terracotta pipes show pottery as one enabling material route, not the only possible plumbing material.
Hydraulic pressure, gravity, flow, and friction enable water-distribution design without making formal hydraulics a hard prerequisite.
Roman lead pipes support lead working as one enabling pipe-material route, not a universal hard prerequisite.
